items.headline: Lebanon, Israel to meet for tough talks in Washington
items.summary: Lebanese and Israeli representatives are scheduled to meet in Washington Tuesday for U.S.-mediated talks on ending the war in Lebanon, but the prospects of an agreement appear slim.

items.headline: Strait of Hormuz blockade: the complex regional realities the US ignores at its peril
items.summary: The pivotal waterway is much more than a global economic choke point. It is a politically and culturally dynamic region with its own dangerous undercurrents.

items.headline: Sanctioned Chinese Tanker Passes Hormuz Despite U.S. Naval Blockade
items.summary: A Chinese-owned tanker under U.S. sanctions has passed the Strait of Hormuz despite the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports that began on Monday. Citing data from Kpler and MarineTraffic, Reuters reported that the vessel had traversed the chokepoint earlier today.…

items.headline: Iran Limits Strait of Hormuz Transits to Friendly Vessels, Tankers Queue

items.headline: Australia's Fuel Crisis Deepens: Petrol Hits Record Highs as Hormuz Blockade Bites
items.summary: SYDNEY — Australian motorists are grappling with the nation's worst fuel crisis in decades, as petrol prices surge past A$2.40 a litre and diesel climbs toward A$3 amid ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz triggered by the U.S.-Iran conflict.
